knowledge_components.name: Understand the terminology to reference monomials and polynomials

complicators.name: Identify and work with monomials as single terms containing coefficients and variables with whole number exponents
Classify polynomials by number of terms (monomial binomial trinomial) and identify the degree of polynomial expressions
Evaluate polynomials through substitution and understand standard form organization by degree

knowledge_components.name: Like terms in polynomials share the same variable factors and can be combined through the distributive property, which allows for systematic addition and subtraction of polynomials

complicators.name: Combine like terms through concrete examples like place value expanded form, other "bases"
Model the grouping and addition of like terms using the area model.
Identify like and unlike terms in polynomial expressions by matching variable parts and exponents exactly, then add/subtract the like terms.
Simplify polynomial expressions containing single and multiple variables and various combinations of like terms, including complex expressions involving nested groupings
Add and subtract polynomials by identifying and combining like terms in horizontal and vertical formats

knowledge_components.name: Polynomial multiplication extends the distributive property to multiply terms and expressions systematically

complicators.name: Multiply monomials by applying coefficient multiplication and exponent addition rules from previous exponent knowledge
Multiply monomials by polynomials using the distributive property to multiply each term
Multiply binomials using systematic distribution methods including FOIL and area model approaches
